steps.title: Spot the elaborating noun phrase
steps.content: The passage shows “something far more extensive” being immediately followed by “network of such committees”—the text explains what the extensive thing is.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon introduces an elaboration that explains what precedes it. Choice A correctly uses this: the colon + “a” clarifies that the “something far more extensive” is “a network of such committees.” A semicolon requires independent clauses (B doesn't provide them), “it was” is redundant (C), and a period creates a fragment (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
